title: UN Security Council Holds Open Debate on Gaza Ceasefire Consolidation
detail: The UN Security Council held an open debate on April 8, 2026, focused on consolidating the Gaza ceasefire and advancing Phase 2 of the Trump administration's 20-point Comprehensive Plan. Deputy Special Coordinator Ramiz Alakbarov told the Council that Phase 2 would be a 'critical step in consolidating the ceasefire in Gaza.' Concern was raised that Israel was withholding nearly $2.5 billion in Palestinian clearance revenue, deepening the Palestinian Authority's financial crisis. The US delegate highlighted Phase 1 achievements — including the ceasefire and return of hostages — while underscoring that Phase 2 would advance 'full demilitarization and reconstruction of Gaza.' The Council session came amid growing pressure to prevent the fragile ceasefire from unraveling following escalating Israeli strikes and the suspension of WHO medical evacuations.

media.caption: UNSC open debate: Consolidate Gaza ceasefire, halt West Bank violence — UN Deputy Special Coordinator

title: WHO Keeps Gaza Medical Evacuations Suspended After Driver Killed
detail: The World Health Organization's suspension of medical evacuations from Gaza remained in effect on April 8, 2026, following the killing of WHO contract driver Majdi Aslan, 54, on April 6. WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus confirmed the death was caused by Israeli fire on the WHO-rented vehicle in eastern Khan Younis and announced an indefinite halt to the medical evacuation program. More than 18,000 Palestinian patients are waiting for medical care abroad that is unavailable inside Gaza. France expressed 'deep concern' about the suspension, noting that medical evacuations are essential as Gazans lack adequate healthcare access within the Strip. The incident adds to a pattern of attacks on clearly marked humanitarian and medical vehicles documented throughout the Gaza conflict.

media.caption: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after Israeli fire kills contractor Majdi Aslan
